request: Let users edit the reported totals and export the figures for the finance team.

problem.value: Finance needs figures that are currently wrong or incomplete at export time, so someone must be able to correct them before they reach the finance team.
problem.evidence: "Let users edit the reported totals and export the figures for the finance team" implies the raw/aggregated totals aren't trustworthy as-is.

current_process.evidence: No mention of how totals are produced or exported today (manual Excel? existing invoicing export?).

success_metrics.evidence: No stated definition of 'done' (accuracy? faster close? fewer finance corrections?).

actors.value: Some internal role (ops/manager/admin) edits totals; finance team is the export's consumer, not necessarily the editor.
actors.evidence: "users edit" vs "export... for the finance team" — the two are described as different actors.

business_objects.value: Unclear which entity holds the 'totals': Invoice amounts, Mission billing, Contract value, or an aggregated report line.
business_objects.evidence: "reported totals" is generic; in this platform totals could originate from Invoice, Mission, or Contract.

business_rules.evidence: No rule stated on who may edit, under what conditions, or whether edits are capped/validated.

workflow.evidence: Unknown whether totals are editable only pre-issuance (draft) or also after an invoice/document is finalized — this is the classic amendment-vs-edit trap.

integrations.value: An export (file or feed) to whatever system/format finance consumes.
integrations.evidence: "export the figures for the finance team" implies an output artifact, format/destination unspecified.

permissions.evidence: No stated restriction on who can edit financial totals or who can trigger/view the export — sensitive by nature.

config_vs_custom.evidence: Platform is multi-client/configurable; unclear if edit rules or export format differ per client.

constraints.value: If totals derive from invoices, French VAT/legal numbering rules likely forbid silently editing an issued invoice's amount.
constraints.evidence: Domain notes: invoicing follows French VAT and legal numbering rules; editing issued figures is regulated territory.

edge_cases.evidence: Retroactive edits, concurrent edits, partial data, or edits after export already sent are unaddressed.

reporting.value: Editing financial totals that feed finance almost certainly requires a who-changed-what audit trail.
reporting.evidence: impact_signal: financial figures + finance-team consumption implies traceability/compliance stakes even though not explicitly named.

risks.evidence: Risk of totals diverging from source data (invoice/mission) after manual edit, or of double-editing already-issued figures.

questions.q: What exactly are the 'reported totals' — invoice amounts, mission billing, contract values, or an aggregated report line — and where do they currently come from?
questions.why: uncertainty × impact: everything downstream (rules, workflow, permissions) depends on which entity is being edited; this is currently unknown on a high-impact slot.

questions.q: Can totals be edited at any time, or only before the underlying invoice/document is issued/locked? If already issued, should an edit create a new versioned/corrected figure instead of overwriting it?
questions.why: uncertainty × impact: invoices follow legal numbering/VAT rules — editing an issued total may require a credit note or amendment rather than an in-place edit, a fundamentally different build.

questions.q: Who is allowed to edit these totals (role/level), and does that differ by client, contract or country?
questions.why: uncertainty × impact: financial edits are sensitive by default and permission rules in this platform are shared across modules — getting this wrong ripples into several screens.

questions.q: Does finance need to see a history of who changed which total, when, and the before/after values (an audit trail), or just the final exported numbers?
questions.why: uncertainty × impact: editable financial figures feeding a finance export strongly implies an audit/traceability requirement, which is a different build than a plain edit+export.

questions.q: What format/destination does the export need (CSV, Excel, direct feed into an accounting system), and on what trigger/frequency (on-demand, scheduled, per closing period)?
questions.why: uncertainty × impact: the export mechanism shapes the technical build and is entirely unspecified.

summary.objective: Allow authorized users to correct reported financial totals before they are exported to the finance team, ensuring the exported figures are accurate and trustworthy.
summary.scope: Likely involves: (1) identifying the source business object(s) behind 'reported totals' (invoice, mission billing, or aggregated report), (2) an edit capability with validation and appropriate permissions, (3) an export function (format/destination TBD) for finance consumption, and (4) very likely an audit trail given the financial/compliance nature of the data.
assumptions: The editor of totals and the finance team are different actors (edit happens upstream of export).
Totals likely originate from Invoice and/or Mission billing data rather than being freestanding numbers.
An audit trail (who changed what, when) will be required given financial data feeding an external team.
The export is a discrete file or feed rather than live system access for finance.
summary.blind_spot: The request sounds like a simple 'edit a field, export a file' task, but if the totals derive from issued invoices, editing them may legally require a credit note or a new versioned document rather than an in-place overwrite — silently allowing direct edits could violate French VAT/numbering rules and break the audit trail finance actually needs.

problem.value: Automatically computed totals are apparently not trusted or complete as-is; users need to correct them before finance consumes the figures.
problem.evidence: 'edit the reported totals' implies the current automated figure is sometimes wrong or incomplete.

current_process.value: Unknown — likely totals are generated automatically from invoices/missions/timesheets today and exported as-is, with no edit step.
current_process.evidence: No description of today's process given.

success_metrics.value: Finance receives accurate, ready-to-use figures with less manual rework or reconciliation.
success_metrics.evidence: Purpose of the export is explicitly 'for the finance team'.

actors.value: An internal user (manager/admin/ops) edits totals; the finance team is a downstream consumer of the export, not necessarily the editor.
actors.evidence: 'let users edit' vs 'export ... for finance team' names two distinct groups.

business_objects.value: Unclear which entity carries the 'reported totals' — invoice totals, mission/timesheet aggregates, or a computed financial report.
business_objects.evidence: 'reported totals' is not tied to a named entity in the request.

business_rules.value: Unknown who may edit, under what conditions, whether a reason/justification is required, whether edits are capped or scoped per client/contract.
business_rules.evidence: No rule stated; domain shows financial rules often vary by client/contract/country.

workflow.value: Unclear whether editing changes the underlying source record (e.g. the invoice itself) or only a reporting layer/snapshot, and whether edit must happen before export or can happen anytime.
workflow.evidence: Sequence 'edit...export' suggests edit precedes export but is not confirmed.

integrations.value: Unknown export format/destination (CSV, ERP feed, email) and whether finance is notified when new figures are ready.
integrations.evidence: 'export the figures for the finance team' names a consumer but not a channel.

permissions.value: Unknown who is allowed to edit totals (any user vs manager/admin only) and who can trigger/view the export.
permissions.evidence: 'users' is generic and undefined; permissions are a known blind spot in this platform.

config_vs_custom.value: Unclear if edit rights/export format should be configurable per client or hardcoded globally.
config_vs_custom.evidence: Platform is multi-client configurable; not addressed in request.

constraints.value: Unknown deadline, and whether edits must respect invoicing/VAT/legal-numbering rules if totals map to invoices.
constraints.evidence: Domain context flags invoicing follows legal numbering and VAT rules.

edge_cases.value: Unhandled: what if an edited total conflicts with the source invoice, concurrent edits, retroactive changes after export, or export re-run after further edits.

reporting.value: If totals feed finance/accounting, an audit trail of who changed which figure and why is very likely required, not just an export button.
reporting.evidence: Finance-facing figures are typically subject to reconciliation/audit; editing a 'reported' (i.e. already computed) total is a classic override-tracking need.

acceptance.value: Not yet defined — likely: user can edit a total, save, export reflects edited value, finance can open/import the file correctly.

risks.value: Risk of edited totals silently diverging from source records (invoices/missions) with no traceability, causing finance to act on wrong figures.
risks.evidence: Inferred from domain pattern: totals usually derive from invoices, which have their own lifecycle and legal constraints.

questions.q: When a user edits a 'reported total', does that change the underlying source record (e.g. the invoice amount) or only a reporting/export layer, leaving the source untouched?
questions.why: high impact × high uncertainty: this decides whether we're patching a display value or triggering invoice-lifecycle/versioning consequences (VAT, numbering, downstream automation) — fundamentally different builds.

questions.q: Which entity holds these 'totals' — invoice amounts, mission/timesheet aggregates, or a separate computed report — and is it one-to-one or one-to-many (e.g. one contract, many invoices)?
questions.why: high impact × high uncertainty: determines data model and whether an edit ripples across multiple downstream documents.

questions.q: Who is allowed to edit a total (any user, manager, finance-only), and is any justification, approval, or audit log required for the edit?
questions.why: high impact × high uncertainty: financial figures shown to finance are typically audit-sensitive; edit rights and traceability change the build significantly.

questions.q: Should every manual edit be logged (old value, new value, who, when) so finance can trust/trace the exported figures?
questions.why: high impact × low completeness: editable financial totals without an audit trail is a common trap and hard to retrofit later.

questions.q: What export format and trigger do you need — on-demand file (CSV/Excel), scheduled feed, or direct integration with a finance/accounting system?
questions.why: medium impact × high uncertainty: affects effort and whether this is a simple export button or a recurring feed with error handling.

summary.objective: Allow authorized users to correct/override reported totals before sending accurate figures to the finance team, in a form finance can consume directly.
summary.scope: Likely scope: an edit capability on totals (currently auto-computed, probably from invoices/missions), with save/validation, plus an export (file or feed) delivering the figures to finance. Depending on answers, this could range from a simple manual-override + CSV export, to a full audit-tracked correction workflow tied to invoice versioning.
assumptions: The 'users' who edit are internal (manager/admin/ops), distinct from the finance team who consumes the export.
Totals originate from an existing computed source (invoices, missions, or timesheets) rather than being entered from scratch.
Success means finance gets figures they can trust and use without manual rework.
An audit trail of edits will likely be needed given the financial/compliance sensitivity of the data.
summary.blind_spot: Editing a 'reported total' looks like a simple field patch, but if it maps to an invoice, French VAT/numbering rules and the invoice lifecycle mean an edit may require issuing a corrected document rather than overwriting a value — and without an audit trail, finance could act on unverifiable, silently-changed figures.

problem.value: Finance needs to correct or adjust figures before sending them downstream, but the current system may not allow controlled edits of computed/reported totals.
problem.evidence: Request: 'edit the reported totals and export the figures for the finance team' — no explanation of why totals need editing.

current_process.evidence: Not stated — unclear if totals are today extracted manually into Excel or already exist as a report screen.

success_metrics.evidence: No measure of success given (e.g. faster close, fewer reconciliation errors).

actors.value: 'Users' (role unspecified, likely finance/admin) edit totals; 'finance team' consumes the export — possibly a different, external audience.
actors.evidence: Request names 'users' and 'finance team' as two distinct groups.

business_objects.value: 'Reported totals' likely means aggregated Invoice amounts (or Contract billing totals), not a standalone report object.
business_objects.evidence: Context: Invoice carries amount source, numbering, VAT; totals are usually derived, not raw fields.

business_rules.value: Unclear whether 'editing totals' means correcting a draft/report figure, or altering an already-issued invoice amount — the latter conflicts with invoice immutability and legal numbering rules.
business_rules.evidence: Domain note: invoices have amount source, numbering sequence, VAT — direct edits post-issuance are normally not allowed; a credit note/amendment is the correct mechanism.

workflow.value: No defined lifecycle for the edit action — is it inline on a draft, or does it require re-approval / versioning if the source invoice is already issued?
workflow.evidence: Context: contract/invoice lifecycles are state-based; editing an issued document is normally a new version, not a patch.

integrations.value: Export target and format unknown — file download, or feed into an accounting/ERP system used by finance?
integrations.evidence: Request only says 'export the figures for the finance team', no destination system named.

permissions.value: Unclear who may edit totals (all users? finance only? admin?) vs who may only view/export.
permissions.evidence: Request says 'let users edit' without specifying role; permissions are a known blind spot per context (approval circuits & rights are shared/cross-cutting).

config_vs_custom.evidence: Not addressed — could be a standard core feature or a client-specific report/edit rule.

constraints.value: Editing invoice-linked totals likely intersects with French VAT and legal invoice-numbering rules, which restrict post-issuance modification.
constraints.evidence: Context: 'invoicing follows French VAT and legal numbering rules' — a recurring regulatory constraint for this platform.

edge_cases.evidence: Not discussed: what if totals are edited after export, or an invoice is already paid, or edits happen concurrently by two users?

reporting.value: If totals feeding finance/legal figures can be edited, an audit trail (who changed what, when, previous vs new value) is very likely mandatory.
reporting.evidence: Impact signal: editing financial/legal totals used by finance implies traceability/compliance need, escalating reporting from low to high default.

risks.value: Main risk: uncontrolled edits to invoice totals could break VAT/numbering compliance or cause finance to work from stale/incorrect exported figures.
risks.evidence: Derived from business_rules and constraints uncertainty.

questions.q: When you say 'edit the reported totals', do you mean adjusting a draft/aggregated report figure, or actually changing amounts on already-issued invoices?
questions.why: uncertainty(high, empty) × impact(high): if it's the latter, invoice immutability and VAT/numbering rules mean this needs a credit note or amendment flow, not a direct edit — a fundamentally different build.

questions.q: Who exactly can edit these totals — any user, finance role only, or an approver — and is the 'finance team' receiving the export internal or external to the platform?
questions.why: uncertainty(high, empty) × impact(high): edit rights and export visibility are commonly forgotten until acceptance testing, and here two distinct audiences are named.

questions.q: Once a total is edited, does it need to go through re-approval / trigger a new invoice version, or is it a free-standing correction with no downstream effect?
questions.why: uncertainty(high, empty) × impact(high): determines whether this is a simple field patch or a full versioning/re-approval lifecycle, per the contract/invoice pattern.

questions.q: Do edits to totals need to be logged (who changed what, when, old vs new value) for finance/audit purposes?
questions.why: uncertainty(medium, inferred) × impact(high): financial figures typically carry compliance obligations; retrofitting an audit trail later is costly.

questions.q: How should the export reach the finance team — a downloadable file (which format), or a feed/API into their accounting system?
questions.why: uncertainty(high, empty) × impact(medium): changes whether this is a simple export button or a system integration with sync/error-handling concerns.

summary.objective: Allow certain users to correct reported financial totals and make those figures available to the finance team via export.
summary.scope: Likely: an edit capability on aggregated/report-level totals (probably invoice or contract amounts), plus an export mechanism (file or feed) delivering these figures to finance.
assumptions: The 'reported totals' relate to Invoice/Contract billing figures within the existing invoicing module.
The finance team is a distinct audience from the 'users' who edit — possibly external to the platform's usual roles.
Editing likely needs to respect invoice immutability rules (post-issuance changes should go through a versioned correction, not a raw field patch).
Some form of change traceability will be required given the financial/legal nature of the data.
summary.blind_spot: The request conflates 'editing a total' with a simple field change, but if the total belongs to an already-issued invoice, legal numbering and VAT rules mean this must be a controlled correction (credit note/new version) with an audit trail — not a direct edit. Building it as a plain editable field would likely have to be redone once compliance requirements surface.
